fundamental_analysis.details: Weak ROE of 4.6%; Conservative debt levels; Weak operating margin of 4.6%; Good liquidity position

consistency_analysis.details: Inconsistent earnings growth pattern; Total earnings growth of -69.8% over past 10 periods

moat_analysis.details: Inconsistent ROE: only 3/10 periods >15%; Low operating margins (avg: 10.4%) suggest limited pricing power

pricing_power_analysis.details: Declining gross margins may indicate pricing pressure

book_value_analysis.details: Consistent book value per share growth (Buffett's favorite metric); Book value CAGR: 4.4%

management_analysis.details: No significant new stock issuance detected; No or minimal dividends paid

details: Using three-stage DCF: Stage 1 (8.0%, 5y), Stage 2 (4.0%, 5y), Terminal (2.5%)

macro_context.market_sentiment: Strongly bullish to start May 2026: S&P 500 surpassed 7,200 (+0.54%), Nasdaq +0.69%, Russell 2000 +2.21%, buoyed by Apple's earnings beat and Middle East peace-deal hopes. April was the best month for S&P and Nasdaq since 2020.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 in an unusual 8-4 split vote; one dissenter wanted a 25 bps cut while three opposed the easing bias language. Kevin Warsh advancing through Senate confirmation to replace Powell — leadership transition adds policy uncertainty.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict has driven a ~19% spike in global energy prices (Strait of Hormuz closure risk); IMF projects world growth at 3.1% with inflation at 4.4%. US-Venezuela tensions, Iran civil unrest, and NATO posture in Greenland add tail risk. Today's Iranian peace-proposal news is providing some relief, with oil pulling back.
key_events: Fed leadership transition: Kevin Warsh Senate floor vote pending
Middle East peace negotiations — outcome could sharply move energy prices
US midterm election cycle increasing policy uncertainty

ticker_news.headline: Tesla's amended 10-K reveals $573M in 2025 revenue from Musk-affiliated companies (SpaceX, xAI) plus $2B xAI investment that converted to SpaceX equity — raising related-party governance concerns
Tesla plans capex exceeding $25B in 2026 (nearly 3× the $8.5B in 2025), expects negative free cash flow for the rest of the year; spending on Cybercab, Robotaxi network, Optimus, AI infra
Tesla Q1 2026 EPS beat ($0.41 vs $0.33 estimate) but revenue came in slightly below estimates; Q1 deliveries 358,023 vehicles, reclaiming global EV sales lead from BYD
Tesla experiencing European sales rebound with significant registration growth after a multi-quarter slump
TSLA stock down ~16% YTD amid robotaxi deployment delays and elevated valuation multiples, though services revenue jumped +42% YoY to $3.75B and FSD subscriptions hit 1.28M (+51%)

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: UBS raised target to $364 (Neutral) on Apr 23 2026, up from $352 on Apr 14. Gary Alexander (Seeking Alpha) upgraded to Buy in late April citing Europe recovery and Cybercab catalyst. Of ~41 analysts: 19 Buy, 16 Hold, 6 Sell. Range: $24.86 bear to $600 (Wedbush Dan Ives bull). ARK Invest outlier at $4,600 for robotaxi bull case. Last sell-side downgrade was Morgan Stanley in December 2025.

web_context.competitor_activity: Tesla reclaimed the global pure-BEV sales lead from BYD in Q1 2026 (358K vs 310K), but BYD continues rapid global expansion (factories in SE Asia, Europe, Latin America) and is nearly fully vertically integrated. BYD's China home-market share is eroding from local competition. Rivian remains the biggest near-term US EV threat with R3/R3X models targeting sub-$50K price points; Rivian produced 42K+ EVs in 2025 and is approaching profitability. In humanoid robotics, Unitree, Boston Dynamics, Agility Robotics, and Apptronik compete with Tesla's Optimus. Ford and GM are scaling back EV programs, reducing near-term domestic competitive pressure.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 earnings reported April 22, 2026: EPS $0.41 (beat; estimate was $0.33), revenue ~$21.4B (slight miss). Next earnings (Q2 2026) expected ~July 22, 2026 (unconfirmed); analyst EPS estimate for Q2 is approximately $0.42. Q2 delivery markets assign 49% probability to 450,000+ vehicles.
